metadata.title: States PCS: West Bengal GK Questions for WBPSC Examinations

questions.question: The Ashoknagar Oilfield is India’s __th discovered oil basin, which is–
questions.explanation: ONGC had spent ?3,381 crore for the discovery of the Ashoknagar oilfield. The discovery of the eighth producing basin of India will play a role for India’s energy security. The production has started from the petroleum reserve located about 47km from Kolkata. The extracted oil is sent to the Haldia refinery of Indian Oil Corporation (IOC).

questions.question: Delhi–Kolkata Road is now known as–
questions.explanation: National Highway 19 (NH 19) is a national highway in India. It was previously referred to as Delhi–Kolkata Road and is one of the busiest national highways in India.

questions.question: How many major railway junctions are there in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Major railway junctions in West Bengal are Howrah, Sealdah, Asansol, Bandel, Bardhaman, Kharagpur and New Jalpaiguri.

questions.question: What is thr other name of Vivekananda Setu?
questions.explanation: Vivekananda Setu (also called Willingdon Bridge and Bally Bridge) is a bridge over the Hooghly River in West Bengal, India. It links the city of Howrah, at Bally, to Kolkata, at Dakshineswar. Completed on 1932.

questions.question: How many countries have their national anthem inspired from Tagore’s works?
questions.explanation: Tagore’s compositions were chosen by two nations as national anthems: India’s “Jana Gana Mana” and Bangladesh’s “Amar Shonar Bangla”. The Sri Lankan national anthem was inspired by his work.

questions.question: In which year All India Trinamool Congress was founded?
questions.explanation: Mamata Banerjee founded the party All India Trinamool Congress (AITMC or TMC) in 1998 after separating from the Indian National Congress, she became its chairperson.

questions.question: Which of these following languages which Bengali has been derived from?
questions.explanation: Bengali, one of the leading Indo-Aryan languages is the official language of West Bengal. It was developed as a language in the 13th century. Bengali language uses the Bengali script which is derived from the ancient Brahmi script. This language has been derived from Magadhi, Prakrit, Pali and Sanskrit. However, besides these three, Bengali has been enriched by borrowing words from several other languages such as Persian, Hindi, Urdu, English, Portuguese, Greek, Arabic, Dutch, Turkish, French, Japanese, Malay and Burmese.

questions.question: During which festival Khemta dance is famously performed?
questions.explanation: Khemta Dance is a popular form of folk dance of West Bengal which was originated 30-40 years ago. This is a devotional type of dance which is performed by professional female dancers. This form of dance is popular and performed during celebrations of birthdays and marriages. It is performed during the popular festivals of Durga Puja and Dol Jatra.

questions.question: What was the real name of Madhabi Mukherjee?
questions.explanation: Madhabi Mukherjee was born on 10th February, 1942 in Kolkata. Her original name was Madhuri. She started acting at a young age and made her film debut as a child artist in Premendra Mitra’s Kankantala Light Railway (1950). She first acted in a lead female role in Tapan Sinha’s Tonsil (1956) followed by Mrinal Sen’s Baishey Shravan (1960), then she acted in Ritwik Ghatak’s ‘Subarnarekha’.

questions.question: Which story by Rabindranath Tagore inspired Satyajit Ray’s film ‘Charulata’?
questions.explanation: Madhabi Mukherjee created a remarkable impact with her performance as Charulata in Satyajit Ray’s film ‘Charulata’ based on Rabindranath Tagore’s big story ‘Nastanirl’. She also displayed memorable performances in Ray’s ‘Mahanagar’ and ‘Kapurush’.
